venue: Hard Rock Stadium, Miami Gardens, USA
h2h_summary: Uruguay and Cabo Verde have never previously met in international football at any level; June 21, 2026 marks their first-ever encounter.
wc_history_home: Uruguay are two-time World Cup champions (1930 as hosts, 1950 with the legendary Maracanazo over Brazil) and reached the semi-finals in 2010, making them one of international football's most decorated nations.
wc_history_away: Cabo Verde are making their historic maiden World Cup appearance in 2026, having qualified for the first time after topping their CAF qualifying group with a 7W-2D-1L record, becoming one of the smallest nations ever to reach the tournament.
group_context: Group H consists of Spain (2nd in FIFA rankings, Euro 2024 champions), Uruguay (17th), Cabo Verde (69th), and Saudi Arabia; with Spain expected to top the group, the Uruguay vs Cabo Verde clash is effectively a pivotal battle for second place, as three points here would dramatically boost either team's knockout qualification prospects.
strengths_home: World-class midfield engine in Federico Valverde capable of goals, assists and defensive cover
Experienced, battle-hardened defensive unit anchored by Giménez and Araújo with Champions League and La Liga pedigree
Rich World Cup pedigree and mental resilience under the tactically rigorous Marcelo Bielsa
strengths_away: Disciplined defensive compactness and tactical organisation earned under Bubista — unbeaten at home throughout qualifying
Dangerous counter-attacking pace through wide areas with Mendes and Livramento capable of catching higher-ranked sides off guard
Historic debut motivation and strong collective spirit built from a 7-win qualifying campaign that included victories over Cameroon and Angola
team_style_home: Under Marcelo Bielsa, Uruguay press high in a 4-3-3, emphasising intense pressing, quick vertical transitions, and defensive solidarity rooted in their traditional 'Garra Charrúa' fighting spirit.
team_style_away: Under Bubista, Cabo Verde deploy a disciplined 4-2-3-1, sitting in a mid-to-low defensive block and exploiting wide channels and pace on the counter-attack, prioritising compactness and collective organisation over possession.
recent_news: Uruguay drew 1-1 against Saudi Arabia in their last outing, while Cape Verde drew 0-0 with Spain in their last game. Both teams arrive at Hard Rock Stadium in Miami on a point, seeing this as a pivotal match in Group H.
key_players_away: Ryan Mendes (captain / forward, all-time leading scorer with 22 goals)
context: Group H consists of Spain (2nd in FIFA rankings, Euro 2024 champions), Uruguay (17th), Cabo Verde (69th), and Saudi Arabia; with Spain expected to top the group, the Uruguay vs Cabo Verde clash is effectively a pivotal battle for second place, as three points here would dramatically boost either team's knockout qualification prospects.
